title: Naseej Threat Pattern
description: Zero-PII threat intelligence object exchanged between bank nodes on the Naseej network. By contract, this object MUST NOT contain names, national IDs, IBANs, BBANs, phone numbers, email addresses, exact account numbers, device identifiers, or raw transaction records. All quantitative values are bucketed or aggregated before inclusion.

pattern_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this broadcast (UUIDv4). Identifies the message, not any customer.

pattern_hash.description: Deterministic SHA-256-derived hash of the normalized, bucketed pattern features. Format: NSJ_<PATTERN_TYPE>_<16-hex>. Same fraud topology at different banks yields the same hash; the hash is not reversible to identities.

typology.description: AML typology detected by the pattern library.

graph_signature.description: Structural shape of the transaction subgraph. Degree sequences and counts only — node identities are positional, never account-derived.

in_degree_sequence.description: Sorted in-degree sequence of the anonymized subgraph.

out_degree_sequence.description: Sorted out-degree sequence of the anonymized subgraph.

is_cross_bank.description: True when the pattern spans more than one institution. No bank routing detail beyond this flag.

velocity_features.description: Bucketed temporal behavior. Buckets, never raw values, so amounts and timings cannot be replayed.

window_bucket.description: Time window over which the pattern completed.

tx_count_bucket.description: Number of transactions in the pattern.

amount_bucket.description: Total flow magnitude, log-bucketed in SAR equivalents.

burst_score_bucket.description: How concentrated in time the inflows were.

risk_score.description: Detecting node's calibrated risk estimate for this pattern, 0–1.

confidence.description: Detecting node's confidence in the typology classification, 0–1. Distinct from risk: a pattern can be clearly a fan-in (high confidence) with moderate risk.

detection_timestamp.description: UTC time the detecting node generated this object (ISO-8601). Rounded to the minute to avoid correlating with a specific transaction.

source_node_id.description: Opaque Naseej network node identifier of the detecting institution (assigned at onboarding). Not a SAMA bank code or routing identifier.

evidence_summary.description: Human-readable, PII-free description of why the pattern was flagged, for the receiving bank's analysts. Free text is screened by the privacy layer's PII detector before broadcast.

privacy_guarantees.description: Machine-checkable attestation of the privacy controls applied before broadcast.

zero_pii_verified.description: True when the object passed the automated verify_zero_pii() check at the source node.

bucketing_version.description: Version of the bucket-boundary configuration used. Hashes are only comparable across nodes using the same version.

k_anonymity_floor.description: Minimum number of accounts that would produce an identical object — patterns below this floor are not broadcast.

governance_tags.description: Routing, retention, and review metadata for the receiving node's compliance team.

sharing_scope.description: Who may consume this object. local_only: source node only. bilateral: source node plus shared_with_node_ids. network_all: all member bank nodes. regulator_only: source node plus regulator/admin nodes.

shared_with_node_ids.description: Bilateral recipients: opaque node ids allowed to read this object when sharing_scope is 'bilateral'. Ignored for other scopes. An empty or absent list under 'bilateral' means no recipients (fail closed).

retention_days.description: Days after which receiving nodes must purge this object.

requires_human_review.description: True when receiving nodes must route matches to an analyst rather than auto-actioning.

regulatory_basis.description: Reference to the legal basis for sharing (e.g. 'SAMA-CFF-3.2'). Free text, no case identifiers.
